title: Top Five Denzel Washington Movies and ‘The Little Things’
format: Sean, Amanda, and guest Shay Serrano trace Denzel Washington's career from St. Elsewhere to movie stardom, argue about his so-called bad movies, review The Little Things, and rank their five favorites.

segments.heading: The case for the greatest living actor
segments.note: bio, versatility, and what makes him Denzel

films.title: Glory
films.note: One of the two supporting roles (with Cry Freedom) that launched him as a movie star; won his first Oscar.

films.title: Cry Freedom
films.note: Played Steve Biko; the other launching-pad supporting role.

films.title: Fences
films.note: Cited as one of the rare times a co-star (Viola Davis) could go toe-to-toe with him on screen.

films.title: Fallen
films.note: Should be a total stinker (Denzel versus a demon) and he sells it anyway.

films.title: Roman J. Israel, Esq.
films.note: A character piece the hosts call transformative but still recognizably Denzel.

films.title: He Got Game
films.note: The basketball scenes double as proof he really played at Fordham under P.J. Carlesimo.

segments.heading: The so-called bad Denzel movies
segments.note: the mid-budget programmers he never phones in

films.title: The Bone Collector
films.note: Sean caught up with it for this episode.

films.title: Virtuosity
films.note: Shay's contrarian pick; Amanda calls it almost unwatchable.

films.title: 2 Guns
films.note: Sean's uncle told everyone it was the best movie he'd ever seen.

films.title: Safe House
films.note: Sean watched it the day he moved to LA; still never bored despite calling it bad.

films.title: The Taking of Pelham 1 2 3
films.note: Amanda watched it over the weekend testing the theory that any Denzel movie works.

films.title: Out of Time
films.note: Shay's favorite: a ticking-clock thriller with Sanaa Lathan.

films.title: The Book of Eli
films.note: Called quality entertainment, not Oscar bait.

films.title: Courage Under Fire
films.note: Same category: solid, not great.

films.title: John Q
films.note: The hostage-drama tearjerker; Shay wanted it on his top five for the father-son scene alone.

segments.heading: The Little Things
segments.note: the HBO Max release the episode is named for

films.title: The Little Things
films.note: John Lee Hancock's 30-years-shelved serial killer script, with Rami Malek and Jared Leto. The hosts agree it's not good but stayed watchable start to finish, mostly on Denzel.

films.title: The Siege
films.note: Reference point for placing his Little Things cop on the moral spectrum, alongside Training Day's Alonzo.

films.title: Training Day
films.note: No. 1: the most fun Denzel performance to watch, villain and all.

films.title: Malcolm X
films.note: No. 2: the best Denzel performance ever, even if it's not his single favorite movie of his.

films.title: Inside Man
films.note: No. 3: the last Denzel-Spike Lee collaboration, and the smartest.

films.title: Remember the Titans
films.note: No. 4: coach-and-dad-energy Denzel.

films.title: Man on Fire
films.note: No. 5: the one where he just gets to kill people.

films.title: Malcolm X
films.note: No. 1: nearly swapped with Training Day, but the exhilaration of this one won out.

films.title: Training Day
films.note: No. 2: proof she loves movie stars more than movies themselves.

films.title: Inside Man
films.note: No. 3: a movie that's as good as Denzel is in it.

films.title: Flight
films.note: No. 4: an addiction performance built almost entirely without dialogue.

films.title: Devil in a Blue Dress
films.note: No. 5: Carl Franklin's Walter Mosley adaptation; called the movie L.A. Confidential thinks it is.

films.title: Malcolm X
films.note: No. 1: framed as a stand-in for the whole Denzel-Spike Lee run (Mo' Better Blues, He Got Game, and Inside Man included).

films.title: Training Day
films.note: No. 2: the one he saw in theaters multiple times just to watch crowds react to the ending.

films.title: Philadelphia
films.note: No. 3: Jonathan Demme's AIDS courtroom drama; Tom Hanks won the Oscar, but Denzel's evolving lawyer is the underrated half.

films.title: Crimson Tide
films.note: No. 4: his Tony Scott pick, a submarine standoff against Gene Hackman.

films.title: The Pelican Brief
films.note: No. 5: nobody else rates it, he doesn't care.

films.title: Mo' Better Blues
films.note: One of the few Denzel movies with real romantic material.

films.title: Much Ado About Nothing
films.note: The Kenneth Branagh Shakespeare comedy: a silly, likable side of Denzel.

films.title: American Gangster
films.note: The one that couldn't live up to the Jay-Z listening session that preceded it.

films.title: The Manchurian Candidate
films.note: His other Jonathan Demme collaboration; an overlooked, unusually dazed performance.

Ads: Netflix FIFA World Cup Launch Edition
